title: Novell and Microsoft: Good for Business?
author: Jennifer LeClaire (NewsFactor Magazine)
abstract: NewsFactor feature analyzing the landmark Microsoft-Novell interoperability and patent-covenant deal and Oracle's concurrent move to offer support for Linux at lower fees than Red Hat. Peter Kastner (VP research director enterprise technology, Aberdeen Group) argues large IT buyers want 'Tier One' support — one throat to choke — and Oracle and Microsoft fit that role.
importance_rationale: Captures a pivotal 2006 inflection point when Linux support/patent dynamics were reshaped by major-vendor moves against Red Hat.
relevance_rationale: Tier One support dynamics remain a live theme in enterprise IT procurement; Microsoft/Novell patent pact shaped years of Linux IP debate.
prescience_rationale: Kastner was right that enterprises favor Tier One support; however Oracle Unbreakable Linux largely failed to dislodge Red Hat, which was later acquired by IBM (2019).
